title: Fast Style Transfer Networks
concept: Fast Style Transfer trains a dedicated feed-forward CNN to apply one fixed style instantly in a single pass, trading the slow per-image optimization loop for a reusable, real-time network.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is being optimized during classic (slow) style transfer — the network weights or the image pixels?

options.text: The image pixels; the network weights stay frozen
The network weights; the image pixels stay frozen
Both are optimized together every step

exercises.explain: Yes! Classic style transfer optimizes pixels through a frozen network.

cards.title: Too slow for video
cards.body: Classic style transfer takes hundreds of iterations per single image — great for one photo, useless for a live video filter running at 30 frames per second. We need something instant.

cards.title: Fast Style Transfer
cards.body: Fast Style Transfer trains a separate feed-forward CNN (a 'transformation network') to directly output a stylized image in ONE pass. This transformation network is trained once, offline, using the same content+style loss idea — but afterward, applying it to any new image is instant.

cards.title: Custom order vs. factory production
cards.body: Classic style transfer is like a custom tailor hand-sewing one suit from scratch (slow, one-off). Fast style transfer is like designing a suit pattern once, then mass-producing identical suits on a factory line (fast, repeatable, but locked to one design).

cards.title: Real-time art filters
cards.body: Popular mobile apps that turn your camera feed into a 'painting' in real time use fast style transfer: a small pretrained transformation network is baked into the app, applying one style (or a few) instantly to every frame.

cards.title: One network, one style (usually)
cards.body: A myth is that a single fast style transfer network can apply ANY style. In the basic version, each trained transformation network is locked to the ONE style it was trained on — a new painting requires training a brand-new network (later 'arbitrary style transfer' methods relax this).

cards.title: Classic vs. Fast Style Transfer
cards.body: The tradeoff between flexibility and speed:

left.text:
Any style image, on demand
Hundreds of iterations per image
Too slow for real-time use

right.text:
One trained style per network
Single forward pass
Real-time, video-ready speed

exercises.prompt: What is the key idea behind Fast Style Transfer?

options.text: Train a dedicated feed-forward network once so it can apply one style in a single pass afterward
Run the classic optimization loop faster using a bigger GPU
Skip the style loss entirely to save time
Precompute every possible output image and store them on disk

exercises.explain: Correct! A trained transformation network replaces the slow per-image optimization loop.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A basic Fast Style Transfer network can apply any arbitrary style at inference time without retraining.
exercises.explain: False. Basic fast style transfer networks are locked to the single style they were trained on.

exercises.sentence: Fast Style Transfer applies a style in a single forward ___ instead of hundreds of optimization steps.
exercises.explain: A single forward pass through the trained transformation network produces the output instantly.

exercises.prompt: Why is Fast Style Transfer suitable for a live video filter app but classic style transfer is not?

options.text: Fast style transfer runs in a single instant pass per frame, while classic style transfer needs hundreds of slow iterations per frame
Fast style transfer uses larger images than classic style transfer
Classic style transfer only works on black-and-white video

exercises.explain: Correct! Speed is the deciding factor for real-time applications.

exercises.prompt: A startup wants a mobile app offering 20 different artistic filters applied instantly to live camera video. Using basic Fast Style Transfer, what does this require?

options.text: Training 20 separate transformation networks, one per style, each locked to its specific look
Training a single network once, which automatically supports unlimited future styles
Running the slow classic optimization loop live for each frame
No training at all, since style transfer requires no learning

exercises.explain: Exactly! Basic fast style transfer needs one network per style. Next: real-world applications of style transfer.
